name: auth
context: Secure Prometheus and Alertmanager HTTP endpoints with web config, TLS, and proxy-layer authentication.
info: Prometheus and Alertmanager are HTTP-first systems, so the operational question is not just "can I query it" but also "who can query or reload it". Prometheus supports TLS and basic authentication through a `--web.config.file`, while many production deployments still terminate auth and certificates at a reverse proxy or ingress. Agents use this node when API calls need credentials, when reloads fail on protected endpoints, or when exposing the UI safely.
body:
# Prometheus And Alertmanager Auth

Prometheus exposes its UI, query API, and management endpoints over the same web server. Official support for HTTPS and basic authentication is configured with `--web.config.file`, whose YAML controls TLS certificates, client auth, and hashed passwords. The same pattern applies to Alertmanager.

```bash
prometheus           --config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml           --web.config.file=/etc/prometheus/web.yml
```

```yaml
tls_server_config:
  cert_file: /etc/prometheus/tls/server.crt
  key_file: /etc/prometheus/tls/server.key
basic_auth_users:
  admin: $2y$10$example-bcrypt-hash
```

Prometheus does **not** provide full multi-user authorization or RBAC inside the server. In practice, operators often put [[nginx/reverse-proxy]] or a Kubernetes ingress in front of Prometheus and Alertmanager to centralize authentication, SSO, and certificate management. Agents should expect either direct basic auth, bearer-style proxy auth, or network-level isolation.

Lifecycle and admin endpoints are separate concerns. `/-/reload` only works when Prometheus is started with `--web.enable-lifecycle`, and admin endpoints such as snapshot and TSDB deletion require `--web.enable-admin-api`. Protect those endpoints more aggressively than read-only query access.

sections.title: Web config file
sections.body: Use `--web.config.file` to load TLS and basic auth settings for Prometheus or Alertmanager. The official HTTPS and authentication docs emphasize that the file is YAML and that client certificate auth should use `RequireAndVerifyClientCert` when mTLS is enabled. Passwords in `basic_auth_users` should be bcrypt hashes, not plaintext.

sections.title: Reverse proxy pattern
sections.body: Many teams terminate authentication and TLS at [[nginx/reverse-proxy]] or a cluster ingress instead of enabling basic auth directly in Prometheus. This is a good fit when Prometheus needs SSO, request logging, IP allow-lists, or consistent certificate rotation across multiple internal UIs.

sections.title: Lifecycle and admin endpoints
sections.body: `/-/reload` is disabled unless Prometheus starts with `--web.enable-lifecycle`. Admin APIs are disabled unless `--web.enable-admin-api` is set. Agents changing [[prometheus/config]] or using snapshot and TSDB maintenance actions should confirm both the runtime flags and the auth path before assuming the endpoint is available.
